creator: Alexander Terp
description:
sort() works on string lists but the < operator rejects string comparisons. This is inconsistent - sort must internally compare strings, but users cannot.

**Repro:**
```
print(["banana", "apple"].sort())  // ["apple", "banana"] (works)
print("apple" < "banana")          // Error: cannot do 'str < str'
```

**Test files:** `bugs/operators/21_comparison_types.rad`, `108_string_order_in_sort.rad`
title: Strings are sortable but < comparison errors
